text.title: terraform-modules-structure Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for terraform-modules-structure. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every Terraform module MUST contain at minimum: main.tf (primary resources), variables.tf (input definitions), outputs.tf (output values), and versions.tf (provider and Terraform version constraints). Optional files: locals.tf for computed values, data.tf for data source lookups.
rule.rationale: Predictable layout lets agents and engineers locate any construct in O(1) — no file scanning required. versions.tf prevents silent provider upgrades that break consumers.

rule.statement: main.tf MUST contain only resource and module blocks. Data source blocks belong in data.tf. Local value blocks belong in locals.tf.
rule.rationale: Keeps main.tf as the authoritative resource inventory; reviewers can audit resource creation without filtering noise.

rule.statement: variables.tf MUST define every input variable with: a description, a type constraint, and a default value where the variable is optional. Sensitive inputs MUST set sensitive = true.
rule.rationale: Missing descriptions break terraform-docs automation. Missing type constraints cause runtime panics on type mismatches. Unmarked sensitive variables leak to logs.

rule.statement: outputs.tf MUST export resource IDs, ARNs, and connection strings needed by callers. Every output MUST have a description. Sensitive outputs MUST set sensitive = true.
rule.rationale: Callers cannot reference internals of a module — outputs are the only API surface. Undocumented outputs force callers to read the source.

rule.statement: versions.tf MUST declare required_version for Terraform and required_providers with source and version for every provider the module uses. Child modules MUST NOT contain provider blocks.
rule.rationale: Provider blocks in child modules cause Terraform to instantiate a duplicate provider configuration, breaking inheritance from the root module and causing apply failures.

rule.statement: Each module MUST have a single responsibility. A module named vpc MUST NOT manage ECS services or RDS instances. A module named ecs-service MUST NOT create VPCs.
rule.rationale: Single responsibility limits the blast radius of changes. A VPC change should never require touching ECS task definitions.

rule.statement: Modules MUST avoid mega-modules. A module with more than 500 lines across all .tf files is a candidate for splitting into smaller child modules.
rule.rationale: Large modules have many more reasons to change, require longer plan times, and make it harder for reviewers to verify correctness.

rule.statement: Module dependencies MUST flow through explicit outputs and inputs. Modules MUST NOT reference each other's internal resources by hardcoded names or ARNs.
rule.rationale: Implicit coupling breaks when either module renames an internal resource. Explicit interfaces survive refactoring.

rule.statement: Modules MUST provide sensible defaults for optional variables, use validation blocks for input constraints, and add descriptions to every variable and output.
rule.rationale: Without defaults, every caller must specify every input. Without validation, invalid inputs surface as cryptic provider errors instead of readable messages.

rule.statement: Use .tfvars files or per-environment directories for environment differences. MUST NOT use terraform workspace as the primary environment isolation mechanism for production infrastructure.
rule.rationale: Workspaces share state backends and make it easy to accidentally apply the wrong environment. Per-environment directories provide strong isolation with separate state files and explicit variable sets.
